retry_reason: Failure is due to memory allocation or ondemand init, not the refcount operation itself. Retrying may be possible but is not a required part of the wrapper contract.
contract_summary: This function allocates and initializes a new cachefiles_object, setting its reference count to 1 (via refcount_set) and returning it to the caller. It also acquires a reference on the associated fscache_cookie via fscache_get_cookie. The caller takes ownership of the returned object and must later release it with a corresponding cachefiles_put_object (or similar) when done. On failure (memory allocation or ondemand init), NULL is returned and no object is created. The function is a creation wrapper that establishes the initial reference; it does not increment an existing reference.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate and initialize a new cachefiles_object, including setting its reference count to 1 via refcount_set. The function also performs auxiliary initialization (lock, list, debug_id, cookie reference) and error handling (ondemand init), but the refcount_set is a core part of establishing the object's lifecycle. It is a creation/set wrapper.
